name: Dr. Sarah Chen
patient_population_notes: High-volume academic practice. Mixed payer — Medicare + commercial, limited Medicaid. Strong representation of stage III/IV NSCLC and metastatic CRC. Runs weekly MDT tumor board with thoracic surgery, radiology, and pathology.
testing_behavior: Currently splits testing across two vendors by tumor type. Estimated 60–70% of eligible patients receive some form of genomic testing. Has evaluated one competitor CGP in past 18 months without committing.

name: Dr. Michael Torres
patient_population_notes: Predominantly stage IV NSCLC and pleural mesothelioma. Academic referral center — receives complex/refractory cases from across Texas and neighboring states. High proportion of patients on clinical trials or IO combinations.
testing_behavior: Active Tempus user — xT for nearly all solid tumor profiling, increasing xF liquid biopsy orders over last 3 quarters for resistance monitoring. Estimated 80%+ of eligible patients receive CGP. One of highest-volume Tempus users in Houston territory.
key_indications: NSCLC (all drivers), mesothelioma, IO resistance monitoring

name: Dr. Jennifer Walsh
patient_population_notes: Academic GI oncology practice. Mixed payer — heavy Medicare and commercial. Significant proportion of complex/refractory CRC and locally advanced pancreatic cases. Participates in NCI-sponsored GI oncology research consortium.
testing_behavior: Currently sends most CRC and pancreatic cases to Foundation Medicine. Estimated 40–50% CGP utilization among eligible patients. Established coordinator-managed ordering workflow built around Foundation portal.

name: Dr. David Park
patient_population_notes: Mixed solid tumor practice — approximately 160 breast and 130 lung cancer patients annually. Moderate payer mix; some safety-net patients from Rush's community health network. Sees a range of stages including early-stage breast and locally advanced lung.
testing_behavior: Splits testing across two vendors by indication. Comprehensive panel utilization is low — estimated 25–30% of eligible patients. No established coordinator workflow for external CGP ordering.

name: Dr. Amanda Foster
patient_population_notes: Predominantly metastatic and locally advanced breast cancer. Strong HER2+ and HR+ patient population. Commercial-heavy payer mix — Houston Methodist serves a high-income suburban demographic. Many patients are enrolled in investigator-initiated breast cancer trials.
testing_behavior: Current Tempus user but under-ordering relative to volume. Primarily orders BRCA germline via Ambry for high-risk patients; somatic CGP is inconsistent and limited to clearly refractory metastatic cases. Estimated 20–25% CGP utilization.
key_indications: Breast (HER2-low, ESR1, PIK3CA, HRD), later-line metastatic, clinical trial enrollment

name: Dr. Robert Kim
patient_population_notes: Primarily stage III/IV NSCLC and limited SCLC. Academic practice with a research orientation — Dr. Kim runs a molecular tumor board and leads an institutional NSCLC biomarker study. Patient population skews toward referred complex cases with prior therapy.
testing_behavior: Used Foundation Medicine historically; currently exploring alternatives after publication quality concerns. Estimated 70%+ of eligible NSCLC patients receive CGP but via competitor. Orders xT + xR combination specifically for fusion-driven cases when available.
key_indications: NSCLC (all drivers, fusions), SCLC, IO biomarkers (TMB/PD-L1)

name: Dr. Lisa Patel
patient_population_notes: Mixed GI oncology practice — primarily CRC and gastric cancer with some upper GI. Academic setting with a two-person coordinator team managing all administrative workflows. Moderate payer mix — significant Medicare Advantage volume.
testing_behavior: Low CGP utilization relative to practice size — estimated 15–20% of eligible patients. Currently uses a regional lab for standard biomarkers (MSI, HER2 IHC) and Foundation Medicine for select comprehensive cases. No dedicated genomics coordinator.
key_indications: CRC (MSI/dMMR, KRAS/NRAS/BRAF, HER2), gastric (HER2, CLDN18.2), PDAC

name: Dr. James Wilson
patient_population_notes: Mixed hematology/oncology practice at Dana-Farber — approximately 280 lymphoma and 240 melanoma patients annually. Academic setting with high clinical trial enrollment. Commercial-heavy payer mix consistent with Dana-Farber's referral base.
testing_behavior: Current Tempus user primarily for melanoma solid tumor profiling. Hematology cases sent to a separate heme-focused vendor. Tempus ordering is stable but transactional — not a champion. Estimated 50% CGP utilization overall.
key_indications: Melanoma (BRAF/NRAS/NF1), DLBCL, follicular lymphoma, IO combinations

name: Dr. Maria Rodriguez
patient_population_notes: Community breast oncology practice with heavy TNBC and high-risk patient load — approximately 120 TNBC and 150 HR+/HER2- patients annually. Mixed payer including significant Medicaid managed care. Resource-constrained setting; Dr. Rodriguez handles prior auth independently.
testing_behavior: Very limited somatic CGP utilization — primarily orders BRCA germline via outside genetics service. Estimated <10% of eligible patients receive comprehensive somatic profiling. No existing external CGP vendor relationship.
key_indications: Breast (TNBC, BRCA somatic, HRD, PARP eligibility), high-risk hereditary, trial matching

name: Dr. Thomas Nguyen
patient_population_notes: Stage III/IV NSCLC-focused practice at a mid-sized academic medical center. Payer mix includes Medicare, commercial, and some Medicaid. Hospital administration has recently applied cost controls to laboratory spending, creating a reimbursement sensitivity.
testing_behavior: Currently uses in-house targeted NSCLC panel (EGFR/ALK/ROS1 hotspots only) ordered through hospital molecular pathology. Misses NTRK, RET, MET exon 14, and other actionable drivers. CGP utilization via external vendor is near zero.
key_indications: NSCLC (comprehensive driver profiling, fusions), IO biomarkers (TMB/PD-L1), ADLT reimbursement story

name: Dr. Emily Carter
patient_population_notes: High-volume gynecologic oncology-focused practice embedded in a large academic center with complex recurrent ovarian and endometrial disease. Substantial trial enrollment and molecular tumor board participation.
testing_behavior: Uses mixed workflow: in-house hotspot panel for initial workup and external comprehensive testing for refractory disease only. Estimated 30–40% comprehensive profiling utilization among eligible patients.
key_indications: Ovarian HRD/BRCA reversion, endometrial MSI/TMB, trial matching

name: Dr. Kevin Shah
patient_population_notes: Large referral GI oncology panel with high molecular complexity and second-opinion volume from the Northeast. Regularly presents molecular cases at disease-specific tumor boards.
testing_behavior: Current Tempus user for selected metastatic CRC cases but still routes many biliary and rare GI tumors through a competing lab due to legacy internal pathways.
key_indications: CRC HER2/BRAF/KRAS dynamics, cholangiocarcinoma FGFR2/IDH1 fusions, ctDNA monitoring

name: Dr. Nina Banerjee
patient_population_notes: Thoracic-heavy practice balancing tertiary referrals and local community transfers with frequent rapid treatment decisions in advanced disease. Works closely with inpatient oncology consult teams.
testing_behavior: Primarily uses institution-targeted panel and external liquid biopsy when tissue is limited. Comprehensive tissue+RNA profiling is inconsistent and typically delayed to second-line.
key_indications: NSCLC comprehensive drivers, SCLC trial eligibility, tissue-liquid reflex workflows

name: Dr. Patrick O'Neill
patient_population_notes: Mixed heme-onc practice with significant tertiary referral mix and frequent complex relapse cases requiring molecularly guided trial placement. Strong evidence-oriented decision style.
testing_behavior: Uses a heme-focused vendor for many assays and sends only selected solid tumor cross-over cases to broader genomic labs. Interested in consolidating vendors if data depth is comparable.
key_indications: DLBCL molecular subtyping, AML mutation tracking, relapse/refractory trial matching

name: Dr. Olivia Martinez
patient_population_notes: Breast-focused academic practice with emphasis on metastatic HER2-low and endocrine-resistant disease in a research-intensive setting. High patient interest in biomarker-driven options and trials.
testing_behavior: Current workflow relies on institutional pathology and episodic outside comprehensive testing. No standardized trigger for broad genomic profiling in later-line HR+ disease.
key_indications: HER2-low therapeutic selection, ESR1/PIK3CA resistance, ctDNA-informed sequencing
